name: ROW_NUMBER
shortDescription: Assign a sequential 1-based ordinal to each row within a partition
description: Ranking window function. Returns sequential row numbers (1, 2, 3, ...) within each partition, ordered by the OVER ORDER BY clause. No ties are recognized — two rows with the same sort value still receive distinct ordinals.

examples.title: Per-department row number by salary
examples.description: 1, 2, 3 ... within each department, ordered by salary DESC
examples.sql:
SELECT name, salary,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rn
FROM emp

examples.title: Top-N per group via inline LIMIT
examples.description: Add LIMIT N inside the OVER clause: N is pushed into the ES top_hits sub-aggregation so only the top-N rows per partition are ranked and returned.
examples.sql:
SELECT name, salary,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 3) AS rn
FROM emp

notes: ORDER BY is REQUIRED inside OVER (ANSI semantics)
PARTITION BY is optional — when absent, the entire result set is one partition
No-ties semantics: distinct ordinals even when sort values are equal
Top-N push-down: LIMIT N inside OVER sets the ES top_hits.size parameter (top-N rows per partition)
